summary: PR #17 should not merge yet. I verified the referenced review comments against head 8dbd799 and they are addressed, but the current PR head still has a merge-blocking issue: the added backend test suite is red when run against the PR code (`PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_bootstrap -q`), failing in the new Cursor backend path.
work_completed: Reviewed PR #17 against `main` with focus on correctness, regressions, security, and tests.
Checked the referenced review comments on `prompts.py`, `bootstrap.py`, `runtime_cli.py`, `cli.py`, and `config_ui.py` against head `8dbd79906ba190e21c999b3329a2c84fa26d1fde`.
Ran targeted and broader verification on the PR head from a separate worktree, including `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_bootstrap -q`.

findings.message: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_bootstrap -q` fails on the current PR head. The failure is `BackendTests.test_cursor_exec_builds_correct_args`, which crashes in `src/control_tower/backends.py:182` because `_cursor_exec()` unconditionally evaluates `result.stdout.strip()`. That means the new backend test coverage introduced by this PR is not passing, so the branch is not merge-ready.

`src/control_tower/cli.py:233` still normalizes the top-level default backend with `str(defaults.get("backend", "codex"))`. If `codex_defaults.backend` is present but null, `tower start` / `tower resume` will pass the literal `'None'` into backend dispatch and fail with `ValueError` instead of falling back to `codex`. This is the same null-handling class of bug that was fixed for agent registry values elsewhere in the PR.

The referenced review comments are otherwise addressed on head `8dbd799`: `src/control_tower/prompts.py` now omits missing custom prompt files from the Tower bootstrap prompt and validates `prompt_file` containment before reading; `src/control_tower/bootstrap.py` validates scaffolded `prompt_file` paths stay within the repo before writing; `src/control_tower/runtime_cli.py` now uses null-safe fallbacks for custom-agent `task_type` and backend selection; `src/control_tower/cli.py` help text reflects backend-agnostic sessions; and `src/control_tower/config_ui.py` now coerces invalid/null backend defaults to a valid choice before prompting.

follow_up_recommendations: Fix the Cursor exec path so it handles absent/empty stdout safely, then rerun `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_bootstrap -q` and confirm the backend tests pass.
Normalize `codex_defaults.backend` in `src/control_tower/cli.py` the same way agent backends are normalized, then add a regression test for `backend: null` in project config.
Do not merge PR #17 until the backend test suite passes on the PR head.
